description_long: This is a round, bulbous ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a small, slightly flared mouth. The surface is predominantly a natural, earthy beige with darker brown and greenish discolorations, likely from age or use. There are three small, roughly formed protrusions spaced around the shoulder of the vase, possibly functioning as rudimentary handles or decorative lugs. The body shows a matte, unglazed finish with visible textural irregularities and some surface staining. The base is slightly recessed, allowing the vase to stand stably on flat surfaces. Overall, the vase has a rustic, utilitarian appearance, suggesting it may be of historical or artisanal origin.
description: A round, rustic ceramic vase with a narrow neck, three small protruding lugs, and an earthy, matte finish with visible stains and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing two of the three protruding lugs and the wide, rounded body with a narrow neck.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a single, prominent lug and the vase's smooth, curved profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying all three lugs and more pronounced surface discoloration and stains.
description_view_3: Side view with a clear look at one lug and the vase's slightly uneven, textured surface.
